title: Perplexity
description:
Compute perplexity of a language model from its predicted token probabilities.

`Perplexity = exp(-1/N * sum(log(p_i)))`

where `p_i` is the predicted probability of the i-th true token.

Lower perplexity = better model. A perplexity of K means the model is as uncertain as a uniform distribution over K choices.

**Example:**
```
probs = [0.5, 0.3, 0.8, 0.2]
perplexity ≈ 4.22
```
constraints: Clip probabilities to [1e-10, 1.0] to avoid log(0)
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def perplexity(probs: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Compute perplexity from token probabilities.

    Args:
        probs: Predicted probability of each true token, shape (N,)

    Returns:
        Perplexity as float
    """
    pass
explanation: Perplexity measures how well a probability model predicts a sample. It equals the exponential of the average negative log-likelihood. Intuitively, it is the average branching factor of the model: at each step, it is as confused as if it had to choose uniformly from perplexity many options. A uniform distribution over V vocabulary items has perplexity V.

test_cases.name: Certain predictions perplexity=1
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
assert abs(perplexity(np.array([1.0,1.0,1.0])) - 1.0) < 1e-6

test_cases.name: Known value
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
result = perplexity(np.array([0.5,0.3,0.8,0.2]))
expected = float(np.exp(-np.mean(np.log([0.5,0.3,0.8,0.2]))))
assert abs(result - expected) < 1e-6

test_cases.name: Lower prob = higher perplexity
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
assert perplexity(np.array([0.1,0.1])) > perplexity(np.array([0.9,0.9]))

test_cases.name: Uniform vocab=10 gives perplexity=10
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
assert abs(perplexity(np.full(100, 0.1)) - 10.0) < 1e-4

hints: exp(-mean(log(p))) is the geometric mean of 1/p values
